name: Date Ice Cream with Almond Crunch Topping
recipeInstructions:
ALMOND CRUNCH TOPPING
Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C). Lightly oil a baking sheet or line with parchment paper.
Combine oats, pumpkin seeds, and almonds in a bowl. Mix well.
In a small saucepan over low heat, combine vegetable oil, honey, apple juice, and salt. Stir until combined and warm.
Pour the warm honey mixture over the oat mixture. Toss to coat evenly.
Spread on prepared baking sheet. Bake, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes or until golden.
Let cool, then pack in an airtight container and place in freezer.
DATE ICE CREAM
Pour milk into saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring often, until only 1 cup remains (about 20 minutes). Remove from heat and let cool completely.
Remove pits from dates; chop into pea-sized pieces. Place half on a plate and freeze.
Place remaining date pieces in blender with cooled milk. Purée until smooth.
Combine egg yolks and brown sugar in a bowl. Whisk vigorously until lemon-colored.
Bring half-and-half to rolling boil in saucepan. Pour over yolk mixture while whisking constantly. Add puréed dates and vanilla, mix well.
Set aside to cool completely. Transfer to ice cream maker and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions. Stir reserved date pieces into ice cream and pack into freezer container. Place in freezer until set (2-4 hours).
Makes 1 1/2 quarts.
